42.reason: [Error: Should read from file pointer 'in' using fscanf, not scanf. scanf reads from stdin, not from the file.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: Should read from file pointer 'in' using fscanf, not scanf. scanf reads from stdin, not from the file.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: Should use fscanf(in, "%s", y) to read from the file, not scanf which reads from stdin]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.125 , reason:  Should read from file using fscanf(in, "%s", y); also condition should check for 1 successful read, not ~scanf
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Reads from standard input instead of the specified file "article.txt". Should use fscanf(in, "%s", y) to read from the file.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [The program reads from stdin using scanf instead of reading from the file "article.txt". This causes incorrect input handling and likely infinite loop or wrong data.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [The program reads from stdin using scanf instead of reading from the file "article.txt". This causes incorrect input handling and likely infinite loop or wrong data.]

90.reason: [Error: 'i' is incremented unconditionally, even when the word already exists (k>-1). This causes the array to have empty entries and messes up the count and sorting.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: 'i' is incremented unconditionally, even when the word already exists (k>-1). This causes the array to have empty entries and messes up the count and sorting.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: i is incremented even when the word already exists, causing duplicate entries and incorrect count]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.125 , reason:  i is incremented for every token, even if word already existed; this causes a to contain garbage and unique count is lost
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Increments the word count for every processed word, including duplicates. It should only be incremented when a new unique word is added to the array (inside the else block).
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Incrementing 'i' only when a new word is added is correct, but due to earlier logic errors (like reading from stdin and broken segmentation), this may count non-words or miss words. Also, 'i' is incremented even if the word was found (which it isn't here due to misplaced increment), but in this code, it's only incremented in the 'else' branch — however, note that 'i++' is outside the 'else', so it runs every time a valid word segment is processed, even if it already exists. This causes duplicate entries and overcounting.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Incrementing 'i' only when a new word is added is correct, but here it's inside the 'if(z[0]...)' block which is okay. However, combined with earlier logic errors, this may cause miscounting. The primary issue remains the use of scanf instead of file reading.]

50.reason: The trailing semicolon terminates the if statement prematurely, causing the else block to execute for alphabetic characters instead of non-alphabetic delimiters.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The trailing semicolon terminates the if statement prematurely, causing the else block to execute for alphabetic characters instead of non-alphabetic delimiters.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [This semicolon creates an empty statement, making the following else clause unconditional. This breaks word segmentation logic.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [This semicolon creates an empty statement, making the following else clause unconditional and logically incorrect. It should be removed to associate the else with the if.]
